hero.blurb: A neighborhood pizza junky judging corner shops with neon signs and all their greasy glory. We map and rank one honest score with supreme accuracy.

styles.heading: Know Your Slice
styles.subtitle: Every review is tagged with a style. Here's the cheat sheet.

about.heading: Dough and Disorder
about.body:
Dough & Disorder is on a mission to hunt down the best pizza in Cleveland  and beyond. We grade in the spirit of the old neighborhood slice shops...with honesty and integrity, plotted on the map so you always know where the good slices are. Pin by pin, we're mapping every great shop until cholesterol wins.

The love of pizza comes from Friday night pizza with the family. Eating at the corner pizza parlor while drinking a cold Coke and playing Pac-man for 4 hours straight. Then the delivery of cheap college pizza to fuel your nightly study sessions. Finally, showing my kids the different types of pizza and seeing their eyes light up when they eat the perfect slice ...there is nothing like it. 

My philisophy is pizza can be good no matter where you buy it. But there is no gas station, franchise, or frozen boxed pizza that can beat a corner mom and pop. Flop or no flop crust, sweet or savory sauce, provolone or low-moisture mozzarella doesn't matter as long as it agrees with my palette. 

I like the cleveland traditional slice and NY Style. The rest I'll rate but they probably won't be as high as my two first loves. In my opinion there are five key variables that can make or break a good slice. The amount of salt, the quality and quantity of sauce, the type of cheese, the length of dough fermentation, and the quality of oven. All of these can greatly affect the score and ranking of a pie.

footer.tagline: Cleveland, OH · the search for the perfect slice

reviews.name: Lil Ronnie's
reviews.verdict: Delicious pizza by the slice shop in Tremont. It is a great alternative to sister shop Il Rione that takes forever to get a table. Only knock on both shops is the Pepperoni is too salty.

reviews.name: Gina's Pizza
reviews.verdict: Big foldable slices with a clean, classic chew.

reviews.name: Swinging Door
reviews.verdict: Crispy bar pie with serious nostalgia points.

styleDefs.name: New York
styleDefs.desc: New York-style pizza is defined by its large, wide slices, thin foldable crust, and a perfect balance of crispy edges and a chewy interior. It traditionally features a simple, flavorful tomato sauce, shredded low-moisture mozzarella cheese, and is baked in a high-temp oven.

styleDefs.name: Cleveland Crimp
styleDefs.desc: The Cleveland Crimp (also known as a pinched or rolled crust) is a signature pizza-making technique hailing from the Italian pizzerias of Parma and the greater Cleveland, OH area. Instead of a flat or hand-tossed outer edge, pizzaiolos pinch, roll, and fold the dough inward along the rim. This creates a distinct, braided rope-like ridge.

styleDefs.name: Neapolitan
styleDefs.desc: Neapolitan pizza is a traditional Italian pizza from Naples, characterized by a soft, thin, chewy crust with high, airy edges (cornicione) that are blistered from being cooked quickly in a very hot, wood-fired oven (around 900°F). Authentic versions use simple, high-quality toppings like San Marzano tomatoes, fresh mozzarella (di bufala or fior di latte), fresh basil, and olive oil, with the classic variations being the Margherita and the cheese-less Marinara.

styleDefs.name: Detroit
styleDefs.desc: Detroit-style pizza is a thick, square-cut pan pizza originating from Motor City. It features an airy, focaccia-like crust with a crackly, fried bottom, a layer of Wisconsin brick cheese that bakes into caramelized crunchy edges, and is finished with signature "racing stripes" of tomato sauce on top.

styleDefs.name: Tavern
styleDefs.desc: Tavern-style pizza is an ultra-thin, cracker-crisp crust pizza cut into small, bite-sized squares (known as a "party cut") rather than traditional triangles. Originating in the Chicago, this style is known for having ingredients, sauce, and cheese spread all the way to the absolute edges of the pie.

styleDefs.name: Sicilian
styleDefs.desc: Sicilian-style pizza features a thick, airy, focaccia-like dough baked in an oiled rectangular pan, yielding a crisp, fried bottom. Traditionally, it is topped with strong cheese (like caciocavallo), tomato sauce, onions, herbs, and breadcrumbs, and cut into square slices.

styleDefs.name: Deep Dish
styleDefs.desc: Deep dish pizza is a famous Chicago-style pie characterized by a high-edged crust baked in a deep, round pan. It is layered with a thick bed of mozzarella cheese, followed by meats and vegetables, and finally blanketed with a generous, chunky layer of crushed tomato sauce.
